description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` (module
  `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator`) implementing the contract in
  [mutagen.mutation_enumeration](.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md).
- Walks the in-scope AST sub-trees from the AST cache; filters by
  `covered_lines`; consults each mutator's `validate/1`; emits
  `[%MutationSite{}]` plus a parallel `skipped` list.
- Determinism: same `{ast_cache, scope_records, covered_lines}` produces the
  same output, byte-identical, run-to-run.
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` covers all requirements.
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs` exercises the
  determinism invariant on randomly generated input tuples.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`,
  `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs`.
- Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1`, `.v2`, `.v3`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.mutation_enumeration.s1` through `s6`.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` (S5)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex` (S5)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex` (S5)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/**` (S4a)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not read source files. All AST data comes from the (mocked-in-tests)
  AST cache per [mutagen.mutation_enumeration](.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md) r6.
- Do not compile or run any mutation. Enumeration produces a plan; execution
  is S5.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] Unit + property tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1`-`.v3`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Commit: fb834cc
Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (new)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (new)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (stub flips only — see Audit-expanded scope below)

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorTest — 11 tests covering scenarios s1-s6 plus site shape and scope-record ordering:
  - "determinism — same inputs produce byte-identical site lists across two runs" (s1/r1)
  - "determinism holds across 10 consecutive runs" (s1/r1)
  - "nodes whose line is outside covered_lines are filtered before validate/1" (s2/r2)  [tag: filtering]
  - "fully uncovered scope produces no arith sites and a no-mutation-candidates warning" (r2 + r5)
  - "a with_swap site that validate/1 rejects appears in `skipped`, NOT in `sites`" (s3/r3)  [tag: filtering]
  - "skipped entries do NOT contribute to the sites list" (r3)
  - "enumeration walks only the in-scope module's defmodule subtree" (s4/r4)
  - "scoped module with only @callback declarations returns no sites and warns" (s5/r5)
  - "enumeration does not call File.read or Code.require_file" (s6/r6)
  - "every emitted site has the full `Site` struct fields populated"
  - "ordering: scope records are walked in input order"
- MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorPropertyTest — 3 property tests over 100/25/50 randomly-synthesised input tuples:
  - "two consecutive enumerations on identical inputs are byte-identical" (r1)
  - "10 consecutive runs are all equal" (r1)
  - "reordering scope_records reorders sites but preserves set equality" (r1 corollary)

Verification stubs flipped:
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1: execute: false → true
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v2: execute: false → true
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v3: execute: false → true

Audit-expanded scope: `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` is outside this ticket's Allowed touches, but it is the spec file that owns the v1/v2/v3 verification stubs. The merge gate explicitly requires "Spec verification passes (`mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1`-`.v3`)" which is impossible without flipping `execute: false → true`. Precedent: mutagen-wrd.1 / .3 / .4 / .5 all flipped stubs in their respective spec files outside their Allowed touches and were APPROVED. This flip mirrors that pattern and is the only edit outside Allowed touches. The orchestrator's scope note explicitly sanctioned this.

Deferred must requirements: none. All six must requirements (r1-r6) have at least one covering test with a concrete failure mode named in the test body (e.g. r2's "Line 15 produces no site" → asserted via length-and-line check on arith sites; r6's "no File.read" → enforced by passing a path that does not exist on disk, which would crash if re-read was attempted).

Summary: MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator.enumerate/4 walks the AST cache (never opens files), narrows each scope record to its module's `defmodule` body via Macro.prewalk, tries each catalog mutator in canonical order on every node, filters by covered_lines BEFORE consulting validate/1, and emits a `%{sites: [Site.t()], skipped: [skipped_entry()], warnings: [warning()]}` map. Determinism (r1) is structural — same inputs, same Macro.prewalk traversal, same mutator order, same content-addressed IDs. The property test exercises this on 100+ synthesised input tuples. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; full mix test suite 216 tests / 0 failures (was 202 before this ticket, +14 new tests).

title: S4b: Mutation enumerator
